minimal_fix_hypothesis: When writing array formulas (MEDIAN/IF combinations) to spreadsheets, explicitly mark them as array formulas using Excel-specific markers or alternative approaches that guarantee formula evaluation upon file opening.

memory_items.title: Array Formula Handling in openpyxl
memory_items.lesson: Array formulas (containing IF/MATCH/SUM within other functions) require explicit array formula markers to evaluate correctly in Excel upon file opening.
memory_items.generalization: When writing complex formulas with logical conditions inside aggregation functions to spreadsheets, verify the target environment supports the formula type natively or use alternative computation methods.

memory_items.title: Formula Evaluation Validation
memory_items.lesson: A formula string written to a cell does not guarantee correct evaluation; testing requires opening the file in Excel or using a spreadsheet library that validates computed values.
memory_items.generalization: After writing formulas to a workbook, validate by reading back computed values if possible, or use libraries with built-in formula evaluation capabilities before finalizing output.

memory_items.title: Number Formatting vs Formula Cells
memory_items.lesson: Applying number_format to cells containing formulas does not guarantee displayed format matches specification, as formatting depends on formula evaluation state.
memory_items.generalization: When specific cell appearance is required (decimal places, comma separators), consider converting formulas to static values after validation, or ensure formatting rules apply to evaluated outputs not formula definitions.

edits.content:
## 6. Advanced Formula Considerations
- **Array Formulas**: Formulas containing IF/MATCH/SUM inside aggregations (like MEDIAN, SUM, AVERAGE) may require special array formula markers (marked as array/cse formulas in Excel). Use openpyxl's array_formula attribute or convert to helper columns if supported.
- **Formula Validation**: Always verify formulas evaluate correctly by either:
  - Testing in target Excel environment
  - Using pandas with openpyxl engine to read computed values
  - Converting formulas to values post-evaluation if required by task
- **Format Consistency**: Number formats applied to formula cells only display after evaluation. If exact visual output is required, consider computing values separately and writing them as constants instead of formulas.
